channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump-China Summit Ends With Limited Achievements and Focus on Long-Term Relations
item.description: US President Donald Trump completed a two-day visit to China without achieving major trade or strategic wins, despite giving plenty praise to Chinese President Xi Jinping. The visit, first by a US pre...

item.title: Delhi Fire Services Urges Government to Recruit 6,600+ Staff Amid Severe Shortage
item.description: The Delhi Fire Services (DFS) has formally requested the Delhi government to urgently increase its workforce, citing a critical shortage of over 6,600 personnel. Presently, the department operates wit...

item.title: Marco Rubio uses alternate Chinese name amid past travel ban
item.description: During U.S. President Donald Trump's state visit to Beijing, attention was drawn to Marco Rubio, the U.S. Secretary of State and former senator, who was previously banned from China in 2020. Observers...

item.title: Frank Luntz criticizes Trump for floating Vance-Rubio 2028 ticket rivalry during midterms
item.description: On Friday, CNN's Brianna Keilar interviewed longtime Republican pollster and focus group expert Frank Luntz about a recent clip of President Donald Trump. In the clip, Trump publicly discussed the pos...

item.title: Sindh removes fixed market closing times to support businesses and citizens
item.description: Sindh Senior Minister Sharjeel Memon announced on Saturday that the provincial government has officially lifted the 9pm closing restriction for shops, markets, malls, hotels, restaurants, food outlets...

item.title: Former RAW Chief Admits Pakistan Remains Diplomatic Force Despite Tensions with India
item.description: Former RAW chief AS Dulat has admitted that India’s long-standing attempts to isolate Pakistan at the international level have not succeeded. Speaking to a British news channel, Dulat said India repea...

item.title: Luzon Grid Back to Normal Operations After Series of Red and Yellow Alerts
item.description: The Luzon grid has returned to normal conditions as of Saturday morning, May 16, 2026, following several days of red and yellow alerts that caused rotating brownouts in parts of Luzon and Visayas. Acc...

item.title: Officials Say Gordie Howe Bridge Opening Depends on Testing Amid U.S.-Canada Trade Talks
item.description: The opening of the $6.4 billion Gordie Howe International Bridge, connecting Windsor, Ontario, to Detroit, Michigan, remains delayed, with Canadian officials citing necessary testing and commissioning...

item.title: Hawks Officer Testifies on Messages and Threats Related to R286 Million Cocaine Seizure
item.description: A Hawks officer, Lieutenant Colonel Nkoana Joseph Sebola, has revealed messages allegedly exchanged by individuals connected to a massive cocaine consignment worth R286 million that was intercepted in...

item.title: British Aerospace Firm Aeralis Enters Administration Amid Delays and Funding Issues
item.description: Aeralis, a British aerospace company developing a replacement for the Royal Air Force's Red Arrows Hawk jets, has entered administration, resulting in approximately 30 job losses. The company had posi...

item.title: Drake releases a three-album set with mixed results
item.description: Drake has released an unprecedented three-album set—'Iceman,' 'Maid of Honour,' and 'Habibti'—featuring a total of 43 songs. The rollout included extravagant publicity stunts in Toronto, from ice bloc...

item.title: Ramaphosa Dismisses Social Development Minister Amid Misconduct Allegations
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has dismissed Sisisi Tolashe from her role as Social Development Minister following a series of allegations regarding misuse of state resources and improper appointments. Tol...

item.title: APPOEMN partners Lagos State to boost professionalism and training in Nigeria’s event industry
item.description: The Association of Professional Party Organisers and Event Managers of Nigeria (APPOEMN) don talk say dem go dey work closely with Lagos State government to make event industry for Naija more professi...

item.title: Trump Suggests 20-Year Suspension of Iran’s Nuclear Program Could Be Acceptable
item.description: US President Donald Trump indicated that he would accept a 20-year suspension of Iran's nuclear program, marking a notable shift from his previous insistence on a complete, permanent cessation of uran...

item.title: US and Nigerian Forces Kill ISIS Deputy Leader Abu-Bilal al-Minuki
item.description: United States President Donald Trump announced that Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, who was considered the second in command of the Islamic State, has been killed in a joint operation by US forces and the Nigeri...

item.title: Large-scale protests planned in London supporting Unite the Kingdom and Palestinian causes
item.description: London is set to host large demonstrations involving thousands of protesters from multiple causes, including the 'Unite the Kingdom' movement and pro-Palestine supporters. Authorities are preparing fo...

item.title: Wendy Valdez Shares Update on Her Son’s Recovery Following Surgery
item.description: Former Pinoy Big Brother housemate Wendy Valdez recently opened up about her son’s recovery after undergoing a successful operation. In her heartfelt statements, Wendy expressed immense gratitude to t...

item.title: PAGASA warns 44 areas may experience extreme heat up to 44°C
item.description: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical, and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) has issued a warning that 44 areas across the Philippines are expected to reach danger-level heat indices thi...

item.title: Analysis of Angus Taylor’s Migration Policy and Federal Budget Impacts
item.description: In the latest edition of Back to Back Barries, Barrie Cassidy and Tony Barry dissect key political developments in Australia. Their discussion centres on the federal budget and opposition leader Angus...

item.title: Anand RK and the Pulitzer-winning Graphic Account of India’s Digital Arrest Scams
item.description: In May 2026, Anand Radhakrishnan, popularly known as Anand RK, became the first Indian artist to win the Pulitzer Prize for Illustrated Reporting and Commentary for 'trAPPed', a graphic investigation ...
